_note: YOU as a named anime/game character (Gojo, Vegeta, Luffy...). This is the GENERATION route of the Face Lab (docs/FACE_LAB.md): IP-Adapter FaceID holds your face while the prompt draws the character's signature look around it. Add your trained identity LoRA (dashboard LoRA field, e.g. carey_face_v2) to lock the likeness harder. For swapping onto an EXISTING picture of the character, use the Face Swap panel (image.faceswap) instead.
template: portrait of a man as {character}, wearing {character}'s signature outfit and hairstyle, {franchise_style}, {scene}, {lighting}, anime key visual quality, sharp detailed face, dynamic composition
negative: text, letters, words, watermark, blurry, low quality, deformed face, extra limbs, different person, photorealistic skin on anime body, mismatched art styles

character.label: Character (e.g. Gojo Satoru, Vegeta, Monkey D. Luffy)

franchise_style.label: Franchise style (e.g. Jujutsu Kaisen anime style)

scene: iconic scene backdrop from the series

rubric.identity: 1-5 — is this recognizably the same person as the reference photos?
rubric.character_fidelity: 1-5 — is the character's signature look unmistakable?
rubric.style_cohesion: 1-5 — do face and art style fuse seamlessly (no pasted-on look)?
